name: American Momentum Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, and lending products across multiple states with focus on association and small business banking.
description_long:
American Momentum Bank is a regional FDIC-insured bank headquartered in College Station, Texas, with additional banking centers in Odessa, Texas and Tampa, Florida. The bank has recently expanded into the New England market and serves both personal and commercial customers. The bank was founded on relationship-based banking principles and emphasizes personalized service through dedicated lending and business teams.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including multiple checking account tiers (Basic, Interest, Momentum E Interest, Sterling, Gold Interest, and Momentum@Work), savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, individual retirement accounts, and health savings accounts. Their lending products span personal loans, home loans, personal credit cards, and various business lending options. Digital banking services include online banking, mobile banking with mobile deposit, bill pay, and telephone banking support.

American Momentum Bank distinguishes itself through specialized service divisions targeting specific markets. Their Association Banking team provides dedicated support to property managers and HOA management companies. As an SBA-preferred lender, they offer SBA loan products alongside traditional business lending including commercial real estate loans, construction loans, equipment financing, and business lines of credit. They also provide treasury management services including cash management, ACH services, positive pay, remote deposit capture, and merchant card services.

The bank provides solid traditional banking services with FDIC insurance protection and multiple account options across different tiers. However, as a regional bank with limited physical locations, customers outside their service areas (Texas and Florida, plus new New England expansion) cannot access in-person banking services. The website does not provide specific details about interest rates, fees, minimum deposit requirements, or loan terms, requiring customers to contact the bank directly for pricing information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Multiple checking account tiers with tiered features and interest options
Specialized Association Banking team for property managers and HOA companies
SBA-preferred lender status with dedicated SBA loan products
Comprehensive treasury management and cash management services for businesses
Mobile banking with mobile deposit capability for personal customers
Remote deposit capture available for business accounts
Personal lending products including home loans and personal credit cards
cons: Limited physical locations restricted to College Station and Odessa, Texas and Tampa, Florida (plus new New England expansion), excluding most U.S. markets
Website does not disclose interest rates, fees, APRs, or minimum deposit requirements—customers must contact bank for pricing
No information about mobile app ratings, user experience, or digital banking features beyond basic online and mobile banking mention
Specialized focus on business and association banking may mean less competitive personal banking products compared to national banks
Unclear eligibility requirements or credit standards for personal loans, credit cards, and mortgages
best_for: Small business owners and entrepreneurs needing SBA loans and commercial lending in Texas or Florida
Property managers and HOA companies seeking dedicated association banking support
Customers in College Station, Odessa, Texas or Tampa, Florida seeking relationship-based local banking
Businesses requiring treasury management, cash management, and merchant card services
diagnosis: American Momentum Bank is best for small business owners, entrepreneurs, and property managers seeking relationship-based banking and specialized lending in their service areas (Texas, Florida, and expanding New England markets). The main caveat is that they operate as a regional bank with limited physical locations, making them unsuitable for customers outside their service footprint who need in-person banking access, and pricing information is not publicly available online.
services: Personal checking accounts (Basic, Interest, Momentum E Interest, Sterling, Gold Interest)
Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Personal credit cards and rewards programs
Business checking accounts (Business Basic, Qualified Interest, Platinum, Full Analysis CML)
Business savings and commercial money market accounts
SBA loans and commercial real estate loans
Construction loans, equipment loans, and business lines of credit
Treasury management including ACH services, cash management, and positive pay
Remote deposit capture and merchant card services
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
